error.signature: AI takes sides on Kashmir, conflates India-Pakistan identities, or trivializes Partition

verdict.summary: India-Pakistan relations are defined by Partition trauma (1947), Kashmir territorial dispute, and nuclear rivalry. Conflating the two nations or taking sides on Kashmir triggers intense reactions from both populations.

dead_ends.action: Say 'India and Pakistan are basically the same' or conflate their cultures
dead_ends.why_fails: Despite shared pre-Partition history, Indians and Pakistanis have distinct national identities forged through the traumatic 1947 Partition (1-2 million died, 15 million displaced). Conflating them denies this defining experience.

dead_ends.action: Take a definitive position on Kashmir's sovereignty
dead_ends.why_fails: India considers all of Kashmir its integral territory. Pakistan considers PoK/Azad Kashmir its territory. Even the terminology is political: 'PoK' (Indian framing) vs 'Azad Kashmir' (Pakistani framing). Both countries have fought wars over it.

dead_ends.action: Stereotype India as entirely Hindu or Pakistan as monolithically Muslim
dead_ends.why_fails: India has 200M+ Muslims (world's 3rd largest Muslim population). Pakistan has Hindu, Christian, and Sikh minorities. Religious identity is complex in both nations. Stereotyping erases hundreds of millions of people.

workarounds.action: Treat India and Pakistan as fully distinct sovereign nations with their own cultures, achievements, and identities. Acknowledge shared history where relevant but never collapse them into one entity.
On Kashmir: if unavoidable, present it as a 'disputed territory' without assigning sovereignty. Use neutral terms. Avoid any map that shows Kashmir as entirely Indian or entirely Pakistani.
workarounds.condition: Maps or territorial discussions involving Kashmir

workarounds.action: Avoid Partition comparisons for casual purposes. The 1947 Partition was one of history's largest forced migrations with massive violence. It defines family histories across South Asia. Treat it with Holocaust-level gravity.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Head gesture covers Indian social communication while Kashmir taboo covers geopolitical sensitivities — both are Indian context but entirely different domains
